explanations.6: The command -v checks if a program exists in your PATH, and >/dev/null 2>&1 silently discards both normal output and error messages so the user doesn't see noise.
explanations.7: This uses ANSI color codes (the \033[0;31m part) to print 'Error:' in red on standard error stream (>&2) so error messages stand out.
explanations.13: docker info is a lightweight command that talks to the Docker daemon and fails if Docker isn't running; it's faster than starting a container just to test if Docker works.
